Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine in blood and urine, low total CO₂, and significant proteinuria, suggesting possible renal dysfunction. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal odor, low specific gravity, bilirubin, protein, and glucose present) further support renal impairment and possible tubular dysfunction. HbA1c is low, ruling out chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend nephrology evaluation for further assessment of renal function and underlying cause.
